name: A.C.P.E.
description_short: A.C.P.E. Federal Credit Union is a Wyoming-based credit union with $73.8M in assets serving 4,751 members since 1957, offering standard banking services from its Laramie headquarters.
description_long:
A.C.P.E. Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 and has operated for 69 years as a federally chartered credit union headquartered in Laramie, Wyoming. The institution serves a community-based membership model and is classified as a standard community credit union without low-income designation. With a single branch location, the credit union maintains modest but stable operations with 15 full and part-time employees and serves approximately 4,751 members.

A.C.P.E. offers core banking and financial services typical of mid-sized credit unions, including checking and savings accounts, ATM/Debit card programs, and loan products. The institution's loan portfolio totals $48.4 million, consisting of real estate loans ($11.8M), other loans ($36.0M), and unsecured loans ($604.6K). Additional services include low-cost wire transfers and international remittance capabilities. Members have access to deposit accounts including share drafts and regular shares totaling $65.6 million in member deposits.

A.C.P.E. distinguishes itself through deep community roots in Laramie and surrounding areas, with nearly seven decades of local operations. The credit union maintains membership eligibility restricted to its designated community rather than operating as an open-access institution. Leadership includes CEO Marcia L Dent and Chairperson James M Dahl. The institution's financial position shows solid equity of $8.2 million and recent net income of $916,716 as of the last audit in September 2024.

As a single-branch institution in a smaller Wyoming market, A.C.P.E. serves a niche regional membership base. The credit union is not part of the Federal Home Loan Bank system and carries standard NCUSIF deposit insurance protections. Prospective members should verify eligibility requirements for their community and understand that services are limited to one physical location, though ATM and online access may extend reach.
services: Money Market and other deposit products
pros: 69-year operating history demonstrates stability and community commitment in Laramie
Federally chartered with NCUSIF deposit insurance protecting member accounts
Offers low-cost wire transfers and international remittance services
Diversified loan portfolio ($48.4M) across real estate, secured, and unsecured lending
Maintains healthy equity position of $8.2M with positive annual net income of $916,716
Strong asset base of $73.8M supports member services and lending capacity
Community credit union model provides localized decision-making and member focus
cons: Single branch location in Laramie limits physical access for members outside immediate area
Community membership restrictions mean eligibility may be limited by geography or organization affiliation
Limited publicly available information about digital banking capabilities or online account management
Smaller institution may offer fewer product options compared to larger regional or national credit unions
No FHLB membership limits access to certain wholesale funding and liquidity tools
best_for: Laramie-area residents and employees of community organizations eligible for membership
Members seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making authority
Individuals needing basic checking, savings, and lending products in Wyoming
Community-focused savers who prioritize credit union ownership model over national bank alternatives
diagnosis: A.C.P.E. Federal Credit Union is best suited for Laramie-area members eligible for community credit union membership who need reliable, locally-based banking services. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—with only one branch location, membership and in-person service access is restricted to the Laramie area, making it less practical for members requiring multi-location convenience.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
